description_long: This object consists of a small cluster of four irregularly shaped rocks, each with a rounded, weathered appearance and a natural, uncut surface. The rocks vary in size, with the largest being roughly twice the size of the smallest, and all display a mottled coloration of gray and brown with subtle reddish undertones. The surfaces are rough and uneven, featuring natural pits, grooves, and slight angularity typical of stones found in outdoor environments. The rocks are grouped closely together, suggesting they could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area. There are no visible markings, engravings, or artificial modifications on the surfaces. The overall form and texture indicate these rocks are solid and heavy, likely composed of granite or a similar dense stone.
description: A cluster of four rounded, weathered rocks with rough, mottled gray and brown surfaces, varying in size and grouped closely together. The rocks have a natural, uncut appearance and display subtle reddish undertones.
description_view_0: Three of the rocks are prominently visible, with the largest rock at the back and two medium-sized rocks in the foreground; the smallest rock is partially obscured.
description_view_1: All four rocks are clearly visible in a row, showing their relative sizes and the flatness of some surfaces.
description_view_2: The rocks are seen from a higher angle, highlighting the rough, pitted surfaces and the close grouping of the cluster.
description_view_3: A side view showing the length of the largest rock and the compact arrangement of the group, with the smallest rock on the left.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
